steps.reveal: Parcere = to spare (infinitive, continuing 'memento' from VI.851)

hints.present imperative: Imperative of 'parcere' would be 'parce' — 'parcere' is the infinitive
hints.future infinitive: Future infinitive would be 'parsurum esse' — 'parcere' is present infinitive
hints.perfect infinitive: Perfect infinitive would be 'pepercisse' — 'parcere' is present infinitive

hints.paro, parare, paravi, paratum: These are the parts of 'parare' (to prepare)
hints.pareo, parere, parui, paritum: These are the parts of 'parere' (to obey/appear)
hints.pario, parere, peperi, partum: These are the parts of 'parere' (to give birth/produce)

steps.reveal: subiectis = the conquered/subjected (dat. pl., 'parcere' takes dative)

hints.ablative: While 'subiectis' could be ablative, 'parcere' governs the dative — spare 'to' the conquered
hints.nominative: Nominative plural would be 'subiecti' — 'subiectis' is dative plural
hints.genitive: Genitive plural would be 'subiectorum' — 'subiectis' is dative with 'parcere'

steps.prompt: What declension is 'subiectus, subiecti' (as a participle/adjective)?

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'subiecti' ends in -i (2nd declension)
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'subiecti' ends in -i
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'subiecti' ends in -i

steps.target: to war down / to crush in war
choices: to war down / to crush in war

hints.to fight: That would be 'pugnare' — 'debellare' means 'to war down/crush completely'
hints.to destroy: That would be 'delere' — 'debellare' means 'to war down/subdue by war'
hints.to rebel: That would be 'rebellare' — 'debellare' means 'to war down/crush in war'

steps.reveal: debellare = to war down (infinitive, parallel to 'parcere')

hints.present indicative: 'debellare' ending in -are is the infinitive, not a finite verb
hints.future infinitive: Future infinitive would be 'debellaturum esse' — 'debellare' is present infinitive
hints.perfect infinitive: Perfect infinitive would be 'debellavisse' — 'debellare' is present infinitive

hints.bello, bellare, bellavi, bellatum: These are the parts of 'bellare' (to wage war) — the simple verb, not the compound
hints.deleo, delere, delevi, deletum: These are the parts of 'delere' (to destroy)
hints.rebello, rebellare, rebellavi, rebellatu: These are the parts of 'rebellare' (to rebel)

steps.reveal: superbos = the proud (acc. pl., object of 'debellare')

hints.nominative: Nominative plural would be 'superbi' — 'superbos' with -os is accusative plural
hints.ablative: Ablative plural would be 'superbis' — 'superbos' is accusative
hints.genitive: Genitive plural would be 'superborum' — 'superbos' is accusative

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'superbi' ends in -i (2nd declension)
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'superbi' ends in -i
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'superbi' ends in -i

translation: To spare the conquered and to war down the proud
